name: Amoud Foundation
mission: Founded in 1998, the Amoud Foundation impacts the local and global communities by helping people help themselves through sustainable humanitarian projects while maintaining trusting relationships. Amoud is an international humanitarian organization that today serves the people in the Horn of Africa. Amoud also does work on the home-front including helping with the 2006 Hurricane Relief and 2015 Garland Tornado recovery.
theoryOfChange: Helping people help themselves through sustainable humanitarian projects and infrastructure (hospitals, wells, schools) to move beyond temporary relief to long-term dignity.
scoreSummary: AMOUD FOUNDATION shows exceptional impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Partnership with Baylor, Scott, and White Health for medical supply vetting

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.amoudfoundation.org/zakat (Source: https://www.amoudfoundation.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible organization that addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through its 'Lift Up A Child' and food package programs [2][3]. Its mission specifically targets vulnerable populations, including orphans and students with disabilities [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, maintaining a Charity Navigator score of 88.5/100 and a program expense ratio of 89.3% [1][5]. Its financial health is robust, supported by 12.7 months of working capital and a perfect fundraising efficiency rate of $0.00 per $1 raised [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Amoud Foundation provides critical humanitarian relief and orphan care through a highly efficient financial model that directs 89.3% of expenses toward its core programs.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity with 12.7 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: Amoud Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting 15 orphan centers and educational facilities for deaf and blind students [5]. The charity reported a total revenue of $11,142,566 and maintains a Zakat-eligible status to facilitate religious giving for its donors [2][5].

areas_for_improvement.context: While operations are efficient, the charity could benefit from <cite id="16">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway to better communicate long-term impact.
With <cite id="8">high working capital</cite>, the organization should <cite id="17">publish triggers for planned deployment</cite> to explain why reserves are held above typical targets.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization maintains high standards of governance and financial accountability.

confidence.data_freshness: Most data from 2023 Form 990 and 2024 Charity Navigator updates
data_gaps: Missing explicit published Theory of Change document
confidence.notes: Strong financial data availability; impact narrative relies on charity's own metrics.

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors with <cite id="2">verified Zakat programs</cite> serving <cite id="15">orphans and needy populations</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: The charity could further improve alignment by <cite id="18">expanding services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="1">28-year track record</cite> and <cite id="10">perfect 100/100 Leadership score</cite> from Charity Navigator.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation could <cite id="16">expand public documentation</cite> of its long-term impact metrics beyond financial efficiency.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly direct, with a <cite id="13">$3.67 cost per beneficiary</cite> and an <cite id="4">89.3% to 89.3% program expense ratio</cite> depending on the reporting period.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="16">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> to move from output tracking to outcome verification.

rich_narrative.headline: Amoud Foundation provides critical humanitarian relief and orphan care through a highly efficient financial model that directs 89.3% of expenses toward its core programs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize extreme financial efficiency and want their Zakat to directly fund orphan care and disability services in the Horn of Africa.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure nearly 100% of their donation reaches the field
Care about specialized needs like deaf and blind education
Prefer established organizations with long-term local presence
Seek to fulfill Zakat obligations through verified channels
giving_considerations: The charity holds significant cash reserves, suggesting high stability
Focus is on direct aid rather than systemic advocacy
Impact is concentrated in specific East African corridors
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking large-scale global advocacy or those who require high-frequency digital impact updates.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Verified outcomes and direct service delivery, though the formal theory of change is still in the developing stage.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation provides direct aid (food, water, health) and institutional support (orphanages, schools) to improve survival and education for vulnerable children.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high financial efficiency translates into actual improvements in beneficiary well-being.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Expanding needs in the Horn of Africa and a high revenue growth rate suggest capacity for more capital.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Somalia, Ethiopia, Djibouti, Sudan, Togo, USA

peer_comparison.differentiator: Amoud Foundation's <cite id="4">89.3% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="5">peer median of 90.3%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The charity directs <cite id="4">89.3% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs, ensuring minimal leakage to administrative costs.
Amoud Foundation reports a <cite id="11">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that public donations are not diverted to marketing or solicitation.
The organization maintains <cite id="8">12.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, providing a significant safety net for long-term projects.

strengths_deep_dive: Amoud Foundation's financial health is characterized by high directness and low overhead. With <cite id="3">$9,535,194 in contributions</cite>, the charity manages to keep <cite id="12">officer compensation at $114,998</cite>, which is a modest percentage of total revenue. This fiscal discipline allows the foundation to achieve a <cite id="13">cost per beneficiary of approximately $3.67</cite>, an exceptional metric for humanitarian relief. Furthermore, the organization has maintained its <cite id="14">Zakat-eligible status</cite> by ensuring funds are directed to the <cite id="15">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories as defined by Islamic principles.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1998</cite>, Amoud Foundation is a specialized humanitarian organization that provides critical support to <cite id="2">15 orphan centers and two schools for the deaf and blind</cite> in the Horn of Africa. The organization operates with a lean financial model, reporting <cite id="3">$9,535,194 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Amoud Foundation is distinguished by its extreme fiscal efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 89.3%</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="5">industry standard of 75%</cite>. This ensures that nearly every dollar donated is converted into direct services, including <cite id="6">water, emergency aid, food assistance, and healthcare</cite> across regions such as Somalia, Ethiopia, and Djibouti. The charity has demonstrated robust financial growth, achieving a <cite id="7">3-year revenue CAGR of 18.6%</cite>, while maintaining <cite id="8">12.7 months of working capital</cite> to ensure operational stability in volatile crisis zones. Its commitment to accountability is reflected in an <cite id="9">overall score of 88.88.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, supported by a <cite id="10">perfect 100/100 score in Leadership</cite>.
